about.html.hero:
Our Story & Legacy

About Nilambur

A premier Islamic institution rooted in the sacred tradition of Nilambur — where timeless Islamic scholarship meets the demands of the modern world.
about.html.intro:
Who We Are

A Beacon of Islamic Learning in Kerala

is a distinguished Islamic educational institution in the historic town of Nilambur, Malappuram district, Kerala — one of the most respected centres of traditional Islamic learning in the region.

The institution operates under the name , established in , registered under Reg. No. , affiliated with .

It honours the legacy of — a towering Islamic scholar and General Secretary of Samastha Kerala Islam Matha Vidyabhyasa Board — whose lifelong dedication to Islamic education remains the guiding spirit of Markaz.

طَلَبُ الْعِلْمِ فَرِيضَةٌ عَلَى كُلِّ مُسْلِمٍ
"Seeking knowledge is an obligation upon every Muslim." — Prophet Muhammad ﷺ (Ibn Majah)
2009
Year of Establishment
about.html.history:
Our Journey

History & Milestones

Foundation
KT Manu Musliyar's Legacy
Late Jb. K.T. Mohammed Musliyar dedicated his life to Islamic scholarship. As General Secretary of Samastha Kerala Islam Matha Vidyabhyasa Board, he laid the foundation for Islamic education across Kerala.
2009
Establishment of Markaz
Markazul Uloomil Islami was established in Nilambur, named in honour of KT Manu Musliyar, with a mission to provide high-quality Islamic education in the Samastha Kerala Jamiyyathul Ulama tradition.
2013
Official Registration
The institution received official registration under Reg. No. 279/13, and affiliation with Jamia Nooriya Arabiya strengthened its academic standing across Kerala's Islamic educational network.
Growth
Expanding Facilities
Markaz expanded its infrastructure — library, computer lab, smart classrooms, residential hostels, and a grand mosque — all funded through generous alumni and community contributions.
Today
A Thriving Community
Markaz today is a vibrant hub of Islamic learning and spiritual development — nurturing hundreds of students who serve as scholars, educators, and leaders across Kerala and beyond.
about.html.mission:
Purpose & Direction

Our Mission & Vision

🎯

Our Mission

To provide a transformative educational experience that roots students firmly in Islamic principles while equipping them with the intellectual tools to navigate and lead in the modern world.

🌟

Our Vision

To be a premier institution of Islamic higher learning — recognized for producing exemplary individuals who serve humanity with compassion, integrity, and profound knowledge.

📖

Our Approach

Our integrated curriculum ensures students perceive the deep harmony between Islamic faith and rational inquiry — producing spiritually grounded, intellectually sharp graduates.

🤝

Our Commitment

We are committed to ensuring that no student is denied quality Islamic education due to financial constraints. Need-based scholarships keep our doors open to all.

about.html.programs:
What We Offer

Academic Programs

01

Hifz-ul-Quran

A rigorous Quran memorization program with strong emphasis on Tajweed and comprehension. Students build a lifelong connection with the Divine word.

Islamic Studies
02

Aalimiyyah Course

An in-depth multi-year study of classical Islamic texts covering Fiqh, Aqeedah, Hadith, Arabic language, and Islamic history.

Advanced Dars
03

Sharee'th & Arts

A comprehensive program blending Sharia studies with arts and humanities — affiliated with Jamia Nooriya Arabiya.

Degree Program
04

Arabic Language

Structured Arabic instruction from foundational grammar (Nahw & Sarf) to advanced literary expression.

Language
05

Contemporary Sciences

Core modern subjects including Mathematics, Natural Sciences, Humanities, and IT taught to exceed state academic standards.

Modern Education
06

Tarbiyah & Character

A holistic spiritual development program — daily prayers, Halaqahs, mentorship, and community service building God-conscious graduates.

Spiritual Development
about.html.facilities:
Campus Infrastructure

Our Facilities

📚

Markaz Library

Extensive collection of classical Arabic texts, Islamic manuscripts, and modern academic resources with quiet study zones.

✦ Sponsored by 6th Batch
💻

Digital Computer Lab

State-of-the-art computers with high-speed internet for digital research, e-learning, and a comprehensive IT curriculum.

✦ Sponsored by 8th Batch
🖥️

Smart Classrooms

Interactive smart boards, ergonomic seating, and digital tools for an engaging modern learning environment.

✦ Sponsored by Markaz Alumni
🕌

Grand Mosque

The spiritual heart of the campus — hosting five daily prayers, Jum'ah, and all major campus spiritual programs.

✦ Campus Centrepiece
🏠

Residential Hostels

Safe, clean, supervised boarding facilities fostering brotherhood, discipline, and lifelong bonds through immersive campus life.

✦ Full Boarding Available
⚽

Sports & Activities

Football, debate clubs, cultural programs, and community service building well-rounded socially aware graduates.

✦ Extracurricular Excellence
about.html.leadership:
Our People

Leadership & Faculty

Our scholars hold dual qualifications in traditional Islamic sciences and contemporary academic fields.

P

Principal Name

Principal
PhD in Islamic Theology, MA in Contemporary Education. Leads the institution's academic vision with decades of experience in traditional scholarship and modern pedagogy.
H

Scholar Name

Head of Islamic Studies
Specialist in Fiqh and Hadith. Al-Azhar University Alumnus. Brings international scholarly credentials and deep expertise in classical jurisprudence.
Q

Chief Qari

Head of Hifz Department
Hafiz-ul-Quran with Ijazah in multiple Qira'at. Has produced hundreds of Huffaz through a proven structured memorization methodology.
A

Arabic Language Head

Department of Arabic & Literature
MA in Arabic Language & Literature. Expert in Nahw, Sarf, and Balagha enabling direct engagement with Islamic sources in Arabic.

academics.html.hero:
Academic Programs

Where profound tradition meets contemporary intellectual rigor.
academics.html.programs:
Department of Islamic Theology

Hifz-ul-Quran

A rigorous memorization program with a strong emphasis on Tajweed and basic comprehension. Students build a lifelong connection with the Divine word.

Aalimiyyah Course

An in-depth, multi-year study of classical Islamic texts, jurisprudence (Fiqh), theology (Aqeedah), and prophetic traditions (Hadith).

Department of Contemporary Sciences

Core Subjects

Mathematics, Natural Sciences, Humanities, and Information Technology, taught meticulously to exceed state and national academic standards.

Methodology

We believe that all beneficial knowledge represents continuous reflection on creation. Our integrated approach ensures students naturally perceive the deep harmony between steadfast faith and piercing reason.

admissions.html.why:
Why Choose Us

A Legacy of

For over three decades, Markazul Uloomi Islami has been shaping minds and souls, combining the depth of Islamic tradition with contemporary academic rigor.

📖
Classical Islamic Curriculum

Our syllabus is rooted in the authentic classical tradition, covering Quran, Hadith, Fiqh, and Arabic linguistics at the highest standard.

🎓
Qualified Scholars & Faculty

Learn from a distinguished faculty of Islamic scholars and academics with credentials from leading institutions across the world.

🏫
Modern Campus Infrastructure

A fully equipped campus in the heart of Nilambur with library, smart classrooms, labs, sports facilities, and a serene prayer hall.

🤝
Community & Character

We nurture not only the intellect but the character — instilling discipline, compassion, and community leadership in every student.

🌐
Strong Alumni Network

Graduates of Markaz hold influential positions across Kerala and internationally — a testament to the quality of our education.

💰
Scholarships Available

Merit and need-based financial assistance ensures that quality Islamic education remains accessible to deserving students from all backgrounds.
admissions.html.programs:
Academic Programs

Choose Your

Foundation · 2 Years
Hifzul Qur'an

A dedicated program for Quranic memorization with proper Tajweed, Tarteel, and recitation skills under expert Qurra.

⏳ 2 Years 🎯 📜
Secondary · 3 Years
Islamic Foundation Course

Comprehensive grounding in Arabic language, basic Islamic sciences, and the classical texts of the Dars-e-Nizami syllabus.

⏳ 3 Years 🎯 📜
Higher · 6 Years
Aalimiyya / Fazilat

Our flagship Aalim program covering all major Islamic disciplines — Fiqh, Tafsir, Hadith, Aqeedah, and Arabic — following the traditional Dars-e-Nizami.

⏳ 6 Years 🎯 📜
Specialization · 2 Years
Takhassus fil Fiqh

An advanced post-graduation specialization in Islamic jurisprudence, Fatwa writing, and contemporary Fiqh issues for Aalim graduates.

⏳ 2 Years 🎯 📜
admissions.html.process:
Admission Process

Four Simple

01
Submit Application

Fill out the online form below with your personal details, academic background, and preferred program.

02
Entrance Assessment

Attend our written and oral evaluation to assess Quran recitation, Arabic basics, and general knowledge.

03
Interview & Review

Selected candidates are invited for a personal interview with the academic committee and department heads.

04
Enrollment & Fees

Receive your offer letter, complete documentation, pay the enrollment fee, and begin your journey.

alumni.html.about:
معاوينا
33YEARS
About Muaawina

A Brotherhood

بسم الله

Muaawina — meaning — is the official alumni association of Markazul Uloomi Islami, Nilambur. Born from the bonds forged in classrooms, prayer halls, and hostel corridors, Muaawina keeps those bonds alive and purposeful.

Our graduates carry the Markaz spirit into every corner of the world — serving as Imams, scholars, educators, doctors, engineers, and community leaders.

🕌

Spiritual Brotherhood

Strengthening bonds forged through shared worship, learning, and campus life.

🌍

Global Network

Connecting Markaz graduates across Kerala, India, and 18+ countries worldwide.

🤝

Community Service

Organising welfare, scholarships, and da'wah initiatives together.

alumni.html.timeline:
Our Journey

Milestones of

تاريخنا

From humble beginnings to a vibrant global network — the story of Muaawina mirrors the growth of Markaz itself.


Know More →
1993
Markaz Established
Markazul Uloomi Islami opens its doors in Nilambur with a vision to blend traditional Islamic scholarship with modern education.
2000
First Alumni Cohort Reunites
The earliest graduates gather for the first formal reunion, planting the seeds for what would become the Muaawina Alumni Network.
2010
Muaawina Formally Founded
The alumni association is officially established with elected office-bearers, a constitution, and a commitment to alumni welfare.
2018
International Chapters Launched
With graduates across the Gulf, Malaysia, and Europe, Muaawina expands globally with active international chapters.
2024
Digital Era: @muaawina_updates
The official Instagram account launches, connecting hundreds of alumni through updates, highlights, and shared memories.

When are admissions open for the next academic year?
Admissions for the 2025–26 academic year are now open. Applications can be submitted online or in person at the admissions office. The last date for applications is typically April 30th.
What documents are required for the admission process?
You will need your previous academic certificates, transfer certificate, birth certificate, two passport-size photographs, and a valid government-issued ID. International students may need additional documentation.
Are scholarships or fee waivers available?
Yes. We offer merit-based and need-based scholarships. Students with exceptional academic records or from financially disadvantaged backgrounds are encouraged to apply. Contact the admissions office for details.
Is hostel accommodation available for outstation students?
We provide comfortable and secure residential facilities for students who are not local. The hostel includes study rooms, communal spaces, and meals. Limited seats are available, so early registration is recommended.
How can alumni stay connected with the institution?
Alumni can register on our Alumni Network portal, attend annual reunions, and join department-specific WhatsApp and Telegram groups. We regularly host alumni lectures and career networking events.
